trade.summary: Spain and South Africa have moderate bilateral trade. Spain exports food products, machinery, and vehicles; South Africa exports platinum group metals, vehicles, fruit, and minerals. Spain is one of South Africa's larger EU trading partners. Spanish citrus and South African fruit create some competition in EU markets.

diplomatic.status: Constructive bilateral partnership through EU-SA frameworks
diplomatic.summary: Spain and South Africa maintain good diplomatic relations through EU-South Africa Trade, Development and Cooperation Agreement frameworks. South Africa's non-aligned stance on Russia-Ukraine, BRICS membership, and ICJ case against Israel creates some friction with Spain's positions but the overall relationship is constructive.

military.summary: Very limited military cooperation. No alliance. Spain participates in EU peacekeeping missions in Africa but limited bilateral military engagement with South Africa specifically.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Spanish infrastructure and energy firms have explored South Africa opportunities. Spain's Endesa and other energy firms have assessed South African renewable energy projects. Spanish banks have minimal South Africa exposure.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade. Spain is interested in South Africa's renewable energy potential and green hydrogen exports. South Africa's platinum is critical for hydrogen fuel cells — an area Spain is developing. Spain's Iberdrola and Acciona are potential investors in South African renewable energy.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some academic exchanges in marine biology, environmental science, and astrophysics (South Africa's SKA participation creates overlap with Spanish astrophysics interests). Development cooperation includes some scientific capacity building.
